reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 6 gates passed. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by requesting data columns via DataColumnSidecarsByRange when the node is missing custody columns for blocks with blobs. The function handleColumnSidecarUnavailability (line 58) returns early when blobsCount > 0 without throwing RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, allowing partial column responses instead of enforcing all-or-nothing as required by spec. This violates the 01e invariant from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 1097: 'All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included.' The severity remains Medium as it affects data availability guarantees for nodes with missing custody data, causing protocol-level inconsistency without direct consensus impact. Code is in active use (not dead code), exploitable via untrusted P2P entry point, and clearly deviates from spec requirements.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included. (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-043, assertion: forall block, resp: any_sidecar_included(block, resp) => all_sidecars_included(block, resp))
